summary: A rectangular, multi-story fortress-like structure with four tall corner towers and an open central courtyard. The exterior walls are layered with dark stone vertical towers at each corner, lighter stone horizontal bands, white slab ledges around floors, and repetitive small windows. The roof of each corner tower is capped with an overhanging layered slab/wood platform; the main building has a flat roof with low parapets and glass/light blocks on top. Ground around building is cleared dirt forming a border.

shape.overall_form: hollow rectangular block enclosing an open central courtyard, with tall towers at each corner

materials.name: glass
materials.role: small windows / roof light blocks (could be glass panes or sea lanterns)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: white/gray slabs used as ledges and roof parapet

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: thin wooden roof layer under tower caps

materials.name: fence
materials.role: small vertical railings or decorative posts (possible but uncertain)

elements: corner towers (4) with overhanging layered slab roofs
three horizontal floors with repeating small windows
open central courtyard with diagonal decorative markings on grass
white slab ledge perimeter at base and between floors
rebuild_hints: Make a hollow rectangular shell about 31x31 blocks with an interior courtyard; center courtyard is open to sky and grassy with diagonal white lines (likely slabs or carpet) across it.

Place substantial square corner towers rising ~12-14 blocks above base; towers use darker stone blocks and have small evenly spaced windows; cap each tower with an overhanging layered roof: outer slab edge, a wooden center, and a small light block on top.

Construct three stacked horizontal bands around the exterior representing floors: dark lower band, lighter middle band (stonebrick or smooth stone), then white slab ledges between floors; repeat small windows in rows for each floor.
Add white/gray slabs around the ground perimeter as decorative foundation blocks spaced evenly; leave a cleared dirt border beyond the slab foundation.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the lighter horizontal bands: look like smooth stone or stonebrick variants; listed as 'brick' with medium confidence but could be smooth stone or polished variants.
Small glass/light blocks on roof and tower tops might be sea lanterns, end rods, or glazed blocks; identified generally as 'glass' with moderate confidence.
Precise interior layout, staircases, and floor thickness are not visible from external views.
Exact dimensions are approximate (31x31 footprint, 16 height) estimated from visible block counts and proportions and may vary by a few blocks.
